channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Undergraduate Research Symposium
item.description: Date: Friday, April 17, 2026 1:00 PM - 3:00 PM - The 2026 Undergraduate Research Symposium will be held at 1:00 PM on Friday, April 17 in the CBIS Atrium. The event, which is free and open to everyone, will feature presentations from undergraduate RPI students who are currently or have previously worked in URP. Any students interested in presenting a poster are encouraged to reach out to their URP coordinator, applications are open now!
item.author: Students

item.title: April Dining Events
item.description: Get ready for a month full of great food, fun, and community highlighted by the always popular Late Night Breakfast on April 30th from 9pm- 11pm! To see all the events coming up in April click the link above. We hope to see you at one or all of these events!
item.author: Students

item.title: RPI Quantum Computing Club Hackathon
item.description: Date: Friday, April 10, 2026 9:00 AM - Sat , April 11, 2026 2:00 PM - Curious where quantum computing is actually headed and what the industry expects from next gen engineers and researchers? Come find out firsthand from leading academics and professionals from IBM, Rigetti, Naval Nuclear, etc. $1500 prize pool. All quantum experience levels. Industry/academic challenges and workshops. Free food. Add a unique project to your resume! Being quantum-aware is a huge advantage companies look for now…
item.author: Students

item.title: We’re Hiring Our 2026-2027 Student Success Center Tutors
item.description: The Student Success Center is currently recruiting 2026-27 peer tutors across a variety of majors. This flexible, paid, on-campus role offers valuable experience in communication and leadership while supporting fellow students. Fall 2026 Success Center Applications are due May 15th: https://surveys.rpi.edu/jfe/form/SV_56fbplo7sUaJRgG
item.author: Students

item.title: RPI Summer Student Employment
item.description: The RPI Pipeline Office is currently accepting student applications to work in part-time and full-time positions on campus, this July, with the 2026 RPI STEP and the RPI PREFACE programs, mentoring pre-college students. Applications will be accepted until all positions are filled.
item.author: Students

item.title: Tutoring in Nason Classroom
item.description: Did you know we offer tutoring from our Success Center Tutors in Nason Hall? We offer Tutoring in the following courses in Nason: Physics I & II- Mondays, 7-9pm CAD - Mondays, 7-9pm CAL I & II -Tuesdays, 7-9pm CS I and Data Structures - Wednesdays, 7-9pm
item.author: Students

item.title: RPI Semiconductor CoLab Special Seminar
item.description: Date: Friday, April 10, 2026 8:30 AM - 11:00 AM - You’re invited to the RPI Semiconductor CoLab Special Seminar at the NFS Auditorium, Albany NanoTech Complex. Shyam Surthi, Distinguished Member of Technical Staff in Advanced NAND Technology, will present “Bits that Persist: NAND Flash Scaling and Opportunities in the AI Era.” Please register by 04/07, breakfast will be provided, register early for transportation requests. Check the link for ID and site access details.
item.author: Community

item.title: Bird & Brine Delayed Opening April 10, 2026
item.description: Bird N’ Brine, located in the Student Union, will have a delayed opening on Friday, April 10, 2026, due to scheduled equipment maintenance. Please stay tuned for updates regarding our opening time that day by following Rensselaer Dining on social media @rensselaerdining
item.author: Community

item.title: QTPiE Emerging Scholar Grant – Call for Proposals
item.description: Queer and Trans People in Education (QTPiE) invites proposals from emerging scholars interested in queer and trans education research. This grant supports collaborative quantitative research that advances equity for queer and trans people in education. Proposals are due Friday, 5/1. Visit https://www.qtpiecollective.org for the full Call for Proposals and details. Questions? Email jcgarvey@uvm.edu
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Libraries are pleased to provide Nexis+ AI
item.description: In addition to classic Nexis Uni, the RPI community now has access to Nexis+ AI, a strategic research solution designed to empower your academic endeavors by unlocking comprehensive insights and transforming raw data into targeted narratives. Seamlessly navigate vast collections of news, business, legal, financial, and company information using natural language search capabilities and intelligent citation-linked summaries. Whether building a thesis or drafting a research report, Nexis+ AI can…
item.author: Community

item.title: Designing Sustainability: From Entrepreneurialism to Environmental Justice
item.description: Wednesday, April 8 | 4-6pm | Student Union Theater (1st floor) Come to this special panel event featuring guest speakers Alexis Goldsmith (Beyond Plastics), and RPI alumni Gavin McIntyre (Ecovative CEO), and Bobbi Wilding (Clean + Healthy Executive Director). Learn about their innovative solutions to sustainability and have a Q&A afterwards. Stay for a fun activity and food!
item.author: Community

item.title: Join RPI’s Artemis II Splash Down/Return Watch Party
item.description: Date: Friday, April 10, 2026 7:00 PM - 11:00 PM - The event is scheduled for Friday April 10, 2026, starting at 7:00 PM DCC 318 (please keep in mind the event time may change due to NASA scheduling). We’ll start with a few exciting, brief speeches and presentations, along with snacks, beverages, and plenty of fun giveaways.
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Student Film Festival
item.description: The RPI 2026 Student Film Festival is UPAC Cinema and the Arts Department’s fifth annual celebration of film at RPI. On Friday, April 10, 2026 we will be showcasing student work in DCC 308. After the reel of short films an award ceremony will take place. Best Picture, Jury Special, Best Cinematography, Best Audio, Best Editing, Best Acting, and Best Screenplay are the awarded categories this year. Join us from 7pm-9:30pm.
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI’s Severino Center Names Inaugural Winners of Ajit Prabhu Catalyst Fund Apex Awards
item.description: The three awards totaling $75,000 recognize the most promising student- and faculty-led innovations at RPI with the highest potential for real-world commercial impact.
item.author: Community

item.title: TODAY: STS Colloquium - Raquel Velho
item.description: Come by at 11:30am in Sage 5711 for a talk with Raquel Velho. The talk draws from Velho’s new project on the social and political history of the Arecibo Observatory (AO). It aims to briefly share the scholarship from the history and sociology of science that is influencing Velho’s thinking about the project — namely, how infrastructure studies and Latin American STS can expand Big Science histories to account for the contextually embedded and geopolitical dimensions of large research facilities.
item.author: Community

item.title: Webinar Friday April 10: RPI Digital Asset Management (DAM) System
item.description: Join Jim Evans, Director of MultiMedia Services, on Friday April 10 from 11am to 12pm, for a live webinar introducing Rensselaer’s Digital Asset Management System. Discover how to access campus‑wide photography, multimedia files, and institutional assets—all in one place. Register now: https://rensselaer.webex.com/webappng/sites/rensselaer/webinar/webinarSeries/register/783d951c6ab8482e9198a7bd6075a700
item.author: Community

item.title: Physics, Applied Physics and Astronomy Colloquium: Dr. Joshua Quinton, RPI, Department of Physics, Applied Physics and Astronomy
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 8, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Title: “First-principles Prediction of Spin Relaxation, Dephasing and Transport From Density-Matrix Dynamics”
item.author: Community

item.title: Rensselaer Faculty and Staff In Person Workshop - Reviewing Supported Polling Tools On Campus for Courses or Meetings
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 21, 2026 12:00 PM - 1:00 PM - In this workshop we will take time to look at a couple polling software tools that are available and supported on campus and discuss the differences. The tools we will be discussing include iClicker and Slido. We will also take a quick look at the free version of Kahoot! (not supported on campus with license but fun!). Please register for this workshop by April 17th as space is limited. Please look in the See section for the registration link…
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Training: Handshake for Faculty/Staff
item.description:
Are you a faculty or staff member hiring for on-campus research or student assistant positions? Consider using Handshake as a recruiting tool! The CCPD will host a training session about using the system on Wednesday, May 20th from 12-12:45pm in their office, DCC 209. Advanced sign-up is required; email Nicole Doran (stongn@rpi.edu) before Wednesday, May 13th to register or learn more!

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
